explicit_text: I am originally from Thailand, where I lived in the Mae La Oon camp. My family moved there seeking a better future and education after leaving Klo Pa. Thailand became my home for many years, especially during my school days at Yaung Ni Oo High School.

explicit_text: I was born in Klo Pa, a place that had very little development when I was young. Because there was nothing there, my family decided to move to Thailand for a better future and education. Leaving Klo Pa was the start of a new chapter in my life.

explicit_text: I attended Yaung Ni Oo High School, which was founded in May 2003 in Mae Kar Key between Thailand and Burma. The school had to move to Mae La Oon camp because of floods. It was a wooden building with a leaf roof, and it had about 500 students and 22 teachers. I enjoyed studying subjects like English and math, and I was part of the New Generation Students' Organisation there.
